contributingFactors: All 3 pods are CPU-throttled at the 400m limit, consuming 400m each (kubectl top shows 400m/400m/400m)
CPU utilization is 200% of request (401m average) vs 50% target — HPA would need ~12 replicas to reach target but is capped at 3
The HPA maxReplicas=3 was set too conservatively relative to the actual load profile
CPU request of 200m with a target utilization of 50% (=100m effective threshold) means scaling triggers very aggressively at low absolute CPU
Pods are healthy (0 restarts, all Ready) but CPU-starved due to throttling at the limit
rootCauseAnalysis.summary: The HPA maxReplicas limit of 3 is too low to handle sustained CPU demand. All 3 pods are CPU-throttled at their 400m limit (200% of request vs 50% target), and the HPA calculates a desired replica count of 12 but is capped at 3, preventing the autoscaler from scaling out further. Cluster nodes have sufficient CPU headroom, so the bottleneck is purely the HPA maxReplicas configuration.

analysis.rootCauseSummary: The HPA maxReplicas limit of 3 is too low to handle sustained CPU demand. All 3 pods are CPU-throttled at their 400m limit (200% of request vs 50% target), and the HPA calculates a desired replica count of 12 but is capped at 3, preventing the autoscaler from scaling out further. Cluster nodes have sufficient CPU headroom, so the bottleneck is purely the HPA maxReplicas configuration.

selectedWorkflow.rationale: patch-hpa-v1 directly addresses the root cause: the HPA maxReplicas ceiling of 3 is preventing the autoscaler from provisioning the ~12 replicas needed to meet the 50% CPU target. The workflow is designed precisely for this scenario — an HPA at its maxReplicas limit under legitimate traffic pressure with sufficient cluster node capacity available. No code-level regression was identified, ruling out the 'when not to use' condition.

kaResponse.analysis: The HPA maxReplicas limit of 3 is too low to handle sustained CPU demand. All 3 pods are CPU-throttled at their 400m limit (200% of request vs 50% target), and the HPA calculates a desired replica count of 12 but is capped at 3, preventing the autoscaler from scaling out further. Cluster nodes have sufficient CPU headroom, so the bottleneck is purely the HPA maxReplicas configuration.

rootCauseAnalysis.summary: The HPA maxReplicas limit of 3 is too low to handle sustained CPU demand. All 3 pods are CPU-throttled at their 400m limit (200% of request vs 50% target), and the HPA calculates a desired replica count of 12 but is capped at 3, preventing the autoscaler from scaling out further. Cluster nodes have sufficient CPU headroom, so the bottleneck is purely the HPA maxReplicas configuration.
causalChain: Signal: KubeHpaMaxedOut — HPA api-frontend is at maxReplicas=3 with ScalingLimited=True
Why? The HPA desiredReplicas=3 equals maxReplicas=3, meaning the controller wants more replicas but cannot create them
Why? CPU averageUtilization=200% (401m actual vs 100m effective threshold at 50% of 200m request), requiring ~12 replicas to satisfy the target
Why? All 3 existing pods are CPU-throttled at their 400m limit (kubectl top shows 400m for each pod), unable to handle additional load
Root cause: maxReplicas=3 is configured too low on the HPA for the actual sustained CPU demand — the ceiling prevents the autoscaler from scaling out to relieve CPU pressure

dueDiligence.targetAccuracy: Targeting HPA/api-frontend because its maxReplicas=3 field is the direct configuration constraint preventing scale-out. An automated workflow can increase maxReplicas to allow the HPA to scale the Deployment further. The Deployment itself does not need modification — the HPA governs replica count.
dueDiligence.proportionality: The fix (increasing maxReplicas) is precisely scoped to the HPA resource. It does not require changes to the Deployment, node pool, or application. The CPU request/limit tuning is a secondary optimization noted in contributing_factors.
dueDiligence.scopeCompleteness: Investigated: HPA spec/status, Deployment spec, all 3 pods (status, resource usage, logs), node CPU capacity, HPA events, Prometheus metrics (current replicas, desired replicas, HPA conditions, per-pod CPU utilization). Did not investigate: network ingress/load balancer to understand traffic source, application-level metrics (request rate/latency). These are not needed to confirm the HPA maxReplicas bottleneck.
dueDiligence.causalCompleteness: Traced from alert → HPA maxed → desired vs current replica mismatch → CPU at 200% utilization → all pods hitting CPU limit → maxReplicas too low. Cannot go deeper without knowing the expected load profile or traffic source, but the configuration constraint is the definitive blocker.
dueDiligence.evidenceSufficiency: All key claims are backed by tool output: HPA describe shows maxReplicas=3, currentReplicas=3, desiredReplicas=3, averageUtilization=200. kubectl top pods confirms all 3 pods at 400m (CPU limit). HPA events confirm SuccessfulRescale to 3 and ScalingLimited condition. Prometheus confirms desired_replicas=3 and ScalingLimited=true condition. Node CPU data from kubectl_top_nodes shows available node headroom.
dueDiligence.regressionAwareness: No prior remediation history available for demo-gateway/HorizontalPodAutoscaler/api-frontend or demo-gateway/Deployment/api-frontend. N/A — no previously failed remediations to regress against.
dueDiligence.alternativeHypotheses: 1) Node CPU exhaustion: Ruled out — worker nodes show 1118m, 3661m, 1453m CPU usage with available capacity. 2) Pod scheduling failure: Ruled out — all 3 pods are Running/Ready with 0 restarts on separate nodes. 3) Metrics pipeline failure: Briefly observed (FailedGetResourceMetric event at 14:19:29) but resolved — HPA is now successfully computing metrics (ValidMetricFound=True). 4) Application bug causing CPU spike: Possible but not the primary issue — the HPA limit is the actionable constraint regardless of whether load is legitimate or not.
dueDiligence.confidenceCalibration: Starting at 1.0. -0.05 for not knowing the expected/normal traffic load profile (cannot confirm if 200% CPU is transient spike vs sustained baseline). -0.03 for not investigating the traffic source (ingress/service) to rule out traffic anomaly as a contributing factor. Final confidence: 0.92.

contributingFactors: All 3 pods are CPU-throttled at the 400m limit, consuming 400m each (kubectl top shows 400m/400m/400m)
CPU utilization is 200% of request (401m average) vs 50% target — HPA would need ~12 replicas to reach target but is capped at 3
The HPA maxReplicas=3 was set too conservatively relative to the actual load profile
CPU request of 200m with a target utilization of 50% (=100m effective threshold) means scaling triggers very aggressively at low absolute CPU
Pods are healthy (0 restarts, all Ready) but CPU-starved due to throttling at the limit
